page: Microsoft Azure HX176-48rs is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the HX family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor. The hardware configuration includes 1408.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of approximately 29.33 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server is equipped with 4332 GB of local storage and is designated as Premium Storage capable. Additionally, the HX176-48rs supports Remote Direct Memory Access capabilities, which facilitates high-throughput, low-latency networking interfaces for clustered configurations. There are no GPUs bundled with this instance type.

From a resource-density perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated CPU allocation position this server for workloads requiring massive memory footprints without the overhead of excess compute cores. The combination of RDMA capabilities, substantial local storage, and high memory capacity makes the HX176-48rs suited for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure HX176-48rs is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the HX family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor, paired with 1408.0 GB of system memory, which provides a high resource density of 29.33 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance includes 4332 GB of local storage, is Premium Storage capable, and supports RDMA networking. No GPUs are included in this configuration. This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads by providing a massive memory footprint relative to its core count. The Microsoft Azure HX176-48rs is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory data analytics, and high-performance computing applications.
